diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute metabolic (lactic-acidosis) crisis of mitochondrial disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Kussmaul respirations are respiratory compensation for a severe metabolic acidosis.
Kussmaul breathing also occurs in DKA — hence the need to check glucose.
Anxiety causes rapid shallow breathing with objective signs of shock absent — it cannot explain the encephalopathy and flaccid weakness.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute metabolic (lactic-acidosis) crisis of mitochondrial disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Altered mental state marks central nervous system involvement in the metabolic crisis.
Encephalopathy is also a feature of sepsis — a reason it must be excluded in hospital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound flaccid whole-body weakness, cannot stand

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute metabolic (lactic-acidosis) crisis of mitochondrial disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute-on-chronic myocyte energy failure with acidosis; a marked change from the walking-with-aids baseline signals decompensation.
Severe weakness overlaps, but the metabolic pattern and known aetiology point away from a primary neuromuscular crisis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vomiting and near-total fasting during an intercurrent viral illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute metabolic (lactic-acidosis) crisis of mitochondrial disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Catabolic stress from fasting/vomiting is the classic precipitant of mitochondrial decompensation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute metabolic (lactic-acidosis) crisis of mitochondrial disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The underlying disorder is the substrate for a lactic-acidosis crisis; without it this decompensation pattern would not occur.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal capillary blood glucose 4.0 mmol/L (home meter)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal glucose excludes DKA and hypoglycaemia as the cause of the Kussmaul breathing.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute metabolic (lactic-acidosis) crisis of mitochondrial disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal glucose does NOT exclude a lactic acidosis — it is a false-reassurance trap; catabolism-halting glucose is still given despite a normal level.
Absence of fever makes sepsis less likely as the sole driver, though it cannot exclude it — normothermia does not rule out serious infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: SpO2 97% with the reported breathing distress

loinc_ref.display: Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute metabolic (lactic-acidosis) crisis of mitochondrial disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal SpO2 with severe tachypnoea is expected — the breathing is compensating for acidosis, not correcting hypoxia; a normal number must not falsely reassure.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute metabolic (lactic-acidosis) crisis of mitochondrial disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective haemodynamic instability — clinician-measured; a remote service infers circulatory compromise from pallor, sweating and drowsiness rather than a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Kussmaul respirations at RR 32 on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute metabolic (lactic-acidosis) crisis of mitochondrial disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician characterisation of the respiratory pattern confirms metabolic compensation; the lay report ('deep fast breathing') is the telehealth-available proxy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral ptosis and progressive external ophthalmoplegia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ptosis and ophthalmoplegia are characteristic chronic features of the underlying mitochondrial phenotype — baseline, not the acute change.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ragged red fibres on skeletal muscle biopsy

investigation_details.expected_result: Ragged red fibres — established the mitochondrial myopathy diagnosis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ragged red fibres are the histological hallmark that confirmed the underlying disorder — historical, sealed, never obtained in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute metabolic (lactic-acidosis) crisis of mitochondrial disease
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Kussmaul breathing + encephalopathy + profound weakness + a catabolic trigger, on a background of known mitochondrial disease, together define a life-threatening decompensation demanding emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies the metabolic (lactic) acidosis driving the presentation — the key hospital investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes, glucose, ammonia, renal function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess anion gap, exclude other metabolic contributors, guide fluid and electrolyte replacement.
Co-existing or precipitating infection must be sought given the GI prodrome, even though afebrile.
Arrhythmia surveillance during acidosis, especially with coexisting hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home coaching of any further self-testing at the scene
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The remote service must escalate, not delay by directing further home tests — none change the immediate need for 000.
